title: Garlicky Molten Vietnamese Pork Bao
description: Crowd-pleasing Vietnamese dish featuring garlicky, molten pork with crunchy bread crumbs wrapped in a lotus leaf.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, bread crumbs, garlic, soy sauce, fish sauce, brown sugar, ginger, and black pepper.
Mix well with your hands until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Preheat a steamer or a pot with a steamer basket over high heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of water to the steamer or pot and cover with a lid.
Place the lotus leaves in the steamer and top each leaf with a portion of the pork mixture.
Steam the pork for 10-12 minutes or until cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the scallions and cook, stirring constantly, until softened and slightly caramelized.
To serve, place each bao on a plate and top with a spoonful of the scallion mixture.
